purpose: The commit fixes a bug in the hci_cmd_sync_queue function by returning an error when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set.
The commit removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h in n_hdlc.c, which is not needed since linux/tty.h is already included.
The commit updates documentation for a new metric 'N/A' added to store operations.
This commit fixes a bug related to vma lock handling during split vma and range unmapping.
The commit adds two new supplies (AVDD and MICVDD) to the rt5682s codec binding.
The commit fixes a resource leak in zorro7xx_remove_one() by adding an iounmap() call to undo the ioremap() done during probe.
The commit removes an unused field from a struct in the net/mlx5e module.
The commit changes the type of cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx from unsigned to signed.
This commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in defconfigs for RISC-V, which should have no functional change but will make the configuration more organized and easier to read.
The commit introduces a new function to check if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode.
This commit adds a new test for the rtc driver that skips when RTC is not present.
The commit removes redundant assignment to a variable, which cleans up clang scan build warning.
This commit decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks, allowing a kernel built without RCU Tasks to still use rcuscale.
The commit removes duplicated text in a comment.
The commit reverts a change that populates a clock table based on dcfclk states, which could cause a no read situation.
The commit adds a new compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC, which has a Bifrost Mali-G31 GPU.
The commit renames a struct to make it more consistent with other names in the driver.
The commit fixes a warning caused by using the wrong format specifier for an unsigned short value in a printf-like function.
The commit fixes a bug related to the range check for the branch instruction on MIPS processors.
The commit removes a case from SetHwReg8188EU() that calls a function. This is part of an ongoing effort to get rid of the unwanted hal layer.
The commit declares non-global symbols as static to fix sparse warnings introduced while adding errata.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from display/msm nodes.
The commit is related to allocating arrays in struct ctrl_ref for v4l2-ctrls.
The commit adds new information to the socinfo driver about various PMICs and SoCs found on boards.
The commit adds support for all possible chip cuts of the Realtek RTL8xxxU wireless driver.
The commit removes a custom software enable count from PRCMU clocks in the UX500 platform.

The commit is related to the initialization of the memory management system for RISC-V processors. It moves a macro called pt_ops to be adjacent to #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL and groups together first type usage cases.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to support I2C nodes.
The commit fixes a typo in a comment and replaces it with a more descriptive phrase.
The commit fixes an infinite loop that may occur if we fail to acquire the HW semaphore, which is needed for resource release.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology for clocking.
The commit removes an unnecessary hack that was used to define IS_SWAPFILE.
The commit adds a new feature to the Linux kernel by implementing GTP echo responses.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate. It also creates a new function called cpumask_first_zero() that was missing.
The commit removes a call to an unsupported firmware function, which prevented TIM information from being retrieved.
The commit adds support for a new platform by adding a board configuration file and connecting the alc5682 headset codec to SSP0.
The commit adds a new feature to the io_uring subsystem by failing zerocopy send requests for protocols that don't support it.
The commit adds a test to ensure that xeCJK.sty is loaded only if it exists.
The commit adds a new feature to checkpatch.pl that allows macros with complex values to be enclosed in parentheses.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with atomic helpers to improve code architecture and enable easier code sharing among drivers.
The commit adds a new interface for jpeg decoding workers to ensure that three HWs run in parallel in MT8195.
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls in libata, which are already covered by tracepoints.
The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank, which is a feature that allows the display to enter a low-power state when not displaying content.
The commit fixes a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure in the kernel by updating the function pointer prototype to match reality.
The commit renames a property from 'reset' to 'resets' in the pinctrl binding for STM32 devices.
The commit fixes a warning generated by GCC 12 due to an array subscript out of bounds.
The commit uses pm_runtime_resume_and_get instead of pm_runtime_get_sync to simplify code.
This commit splits out fixed file installation and removal from io_uring and moves it to the filetable code.
The commit adds support for separate nodes for G1 and G2 decoders with vpu-blk-ctrl power-domain support.
The commit removes a macro that was only used once in the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro.
